responses.question: In a typical week, what is the total number of hours you work across all your roles combined?
How many hours per week do you spend face-to-face (or on calls) with customers — across presales and delivery?
What are the main activities that make up your customer-facing time?
responses.answer: NA (no cutomer face time on delivery. Only cutomer face time is during customer visits)

responses.question: How many hours per week do you typically spend on your Cloud Leader responsibilities?
In your Cloud Leader role, what are the top 3 activities where you spend most of your time and feel you add real value?
responses.answer:
1. Business Pipeline view (Demand and Supply forcast and operations) (collab with BP and Ops)
2. Delivery Governance (Project Health, Faster delivery Initiatives, etc) (collab with DP and Projects)
3. Initiatives - Review and Govern Initiatives - Talent (Enablements, hiring, assessments), OneSaleforce, Innovation

~~~ Roughly 5 hours per week for each of the above 3 activities per cloud (total ~15 hours for top 3 activies per week per cloud)

2x clouds = 30 hours per week

responses.question: Still in your Cloud Leader role — what are 2 activities you regularly have to do, but feel are low-value or unproductive?
responses.answer: Staffing Operations (overhead due BP/DP/Ops loops CLs by default) - Its Manageble/Understandable. It will be enough (good) if CL is looped for HIR/HVR or for Governance related topics which needs CL support.

responses.question: What is the single biggest thing that prevents you from spending time on what matters most in your role?
responses.answer:
Not applicable - Its manageble ~ with Prioritization and Delegation.
Post Script - Too many initiatives per person. But its Addressed by Prioritization and Delegation. It may lead to non availablity of Cloud leader on low priority or tasks delegated to associates and I feel its right.

responses.question: If you could redesign your week, what would you spend significantly more time on — and what would you hand off or stop doing?
responses.answer:
I feel it is balanced rightly for CL role... spending time orderly on 
1) Business (pipe, presales, supply, demand, forecast)
2) Delivery (Projects, Governance, Success, AI led)
3) Talent and People (1:1s)
4) Team, Org and External meetings
5) Initiatives (OneSalesforce, Innovation, Branding)

~ backed by right level of Prioritization and Delegation.
